message:
fix(fcp-webhook): use constant-time HMAC verification + utility crate improvements

fcp-webhook signature verification security hardening:
- Replace string comparison with HMAC verify_slice() for constant-time
  signature verification, eliminating timing side-channel vulnerability
- Properly decode hex signatures before verification instead of
  comparing hex strings
- Add support for "v0=" prefixed signatures (Slack-style format)
- Simplify verify_signature() return type to Result<(), WebhookError>
- Remove unnecessary intermediate hex encoding step

The previous implementation compared signatures as hex strings:
  computed_hex == provided_hex  // timing-vulnerable
Now uses cryptographic constant-time comparison:
  mac.verify_slice(&sig_bytes)  // constant-time

fcp-telemetry formatting improvements:
- Standardize test function formatting in context.rs
- Clean up logging.rs test assertions

fcp-testkit mock server:
- Minor formatting consistency improvements

fcp-manifest dependency additions:
- Add required dependencies for manifest processing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
